meta.title: Drug resistance in post-stroke epilepsy (Lattanzi nomogram)
meta.authors: Lattanzi S, Meletti S, Trinka E, et al.
meta.outcome: Probability of drug-resistant epilepsy (ILAE definition) in people with post-stroke epilepsy, from 5 factors
meta.cff_info:
What this shows. A nomogram (Lattanzi et al., J Clin Med 2023) estimating the probability of drug-resistant epilepsy (ILAE 2010 definition) in people with post-stroke epilepsy. Single-centre, 164 patients (drug-resistance rate 20%; AUC 0.89).

How to read it

  • Strongest driver: a short latency from stroke to first seizure (0–6 months). Also: status epilepticus at onset, intracerebral haemorrhage (vs ischaemic), severe stroke (NIHSS ≥16); older age is protective.

Single-centre model without external validation. Note: the source paper's printed equation accidentally omitted the status-epilepticus term — it is included here (β 2.648, as listed in the paper's own coefficient table). Demonstration tool.

predictors.name: Latency from stroke to first seizure
predictors.assess: Time from the stroke to the first unprovoked seizure. Shorter latency strongly raises drug-resistance risk (0–6 mo: OR ~99 vs >12 mo).

predictors.name: Status epilepticus at epilepsy onset
predictors.assess: Status epilepticus at the onset of post-stroke epilepsy. Present raises risk (OR 14.1).

predictors.name: Stroke type
predictors.assess: Haemorrhagic (ICH) vs ischaemic stroke. ICH raises risk (OR 6.29).

predictors.name: Severe stroke (NIHSS ≥ 16)
predictors.assess: Baseline stroke severity NIHSS ≥16. Present raises risk (OR 4.73).

predictors.name: Age at stroke onset
predictors.assess: Age at the stroke (years). Older age is protective (OR 0.94 per year).

model.note: Post-stroke epilepsy = ≥1 unprovoked seizure >7 days after ischaemic stroke or ICH. Single-centre (n=164). The status-epilepticus term (β 2.648), erroneously dropped from the paper's printed formula, is included.
model.viz_info: Each factor adds its published log-odds to a logistic model; the sum plus the intercept gives the probability of drug-resistant epilepsy.

provenance.source: Lattanzi 2023 J Clin Med Table 3 (β, intercept) — doi 10.3390/jcm12113610, PMID 37297805; SE term re-added (paper's printed formula omitted it)
provenance.notes: Logistic intercept -1.088; β: age -0.061/yr, ICH 1.839, severe(NIHSS≥16) 1.553, latency 7-12mo 1.506 / 0-6mo 4.596 (ref >12mo), status-epilepticus 2.648. NB paper's printed equation dropped the SE term — re-added per its own coef table. Ref patient (ischaemic/non-severe/>12mo/no-SE/age57)→1.0%. AUC 0.894. Built 2026-07-08.
